contact_form: On the contact hypersurface B=0, Q = -S D(B)/24^(w/2).

definitions.B: 24^(w/2) A_w - A_w(1) S^(w/2), the violation/barrier polynomial
definitions.N: x_0 partial_0 A_w - x_1 partial_1 A_w
definitions.Q: w A_w Delta - S N, the first-difference quotient of the pairwise Schur obstruction

identity: 24^(w/2) Q = w Delta B - S D(B)
name: Barrier contact derivative identity
proof_use: The ordered-branch target Q>=0 on B>=0 reduces at first contact to D(B)<=0. This is the exact normal-derivative version of Barrier Schur Compression.
remaining_target: Prove that no first positive crossing of B can have D(B)>0; equivalently prove inward/contracting normal derivative on the contact barrier.
why_this_matters: The barrier condition is now attached directly to the derivative of the barrier polynomial, instead of an unrelated residual polynomial.
